prompt:
Help frame the research described in the benchmark case as a concrete business-research problem. Preserve the supplied model and identify claims or information that remain unsupported.

Condition instructions:
Use the current frame-business-research-problem Skill at skills/frame-business-research-problem/. Follow its readiness, fidelity, managerial-framing, scholarly-positioning, evidence, boundary, and mode instructions. Use only the serialized case material.

Benchmark case:
```json
{
  "adversarial": true,
  "decision_time_facts": [
    "product revenues",
    "assortment capacity",
    "estimated within-store transition matrix"
  ],
  "domain": "OM",
  "evidence_needs": [
    {
      "claim": "The transition matrix represents within-store substitution",
      "source_needed": false,
      "status": "model_assumption"
    },
    {
      "claim": "Customers visit the nearest competitor after an unavailable item",
      "source_needed": true,
      "status": "unsupported_claim"
    }
  ],
  "fidelity_requirements": [
    "Substitution remains within the focal store",
    "No competitor search or store-location choice is modeled"
  ],
  "hard_failure_traps": [
    "model_changed_for_story",
    "hidden_major_assumption",
    "unsupported_causal_claim"
  ],
  "id": "om-11",
  "model_specification": {
    "constraints": [
      "focal-store assortment capacity"
    ],
    "decision_variables": [
      "products offered in the focal store"
    ],
    "mathematical_objective": "maximize expected store revenue",
    "mechanism": "An unavailable product triggers modeled transitions among alternatives inside the focal store until an offered product is purchased or the customer exits.",
    "model_type": "single-store Markov-chain assortment optimization",
    "state_variables": [
      "customer starting product state",
      "within-store transition path",
      "purchase or exit outcome"
    ],
    "uncertainty": "customer starting state and within-store transition path"
  },
  "prohibited_inferences": [
    "An unavailable item sends the customer to the nearest competing store",
    "The retailer controls the realized transition path"
  ],
  "prompt": "A retailer chooses one store's assortment before demand. The model sends demand for an unavailable item through a within-store Markov substitution chain until another offered item is purchased or the customer exits. A proposed story instead says customers search for and visit the nearest competing store. Diagnose the mechanism mismatch without adding store-search or location choice.",
  "tags": [
    "assortment",
    "markov-substitution",
    "store-search",
    "adversarial"
  ],
  "title": "Within-store substitution miscast as nearest-store search"
}
```
